description_long: This is a medieval-style battle axe featuring a long, straight wooden handle with a natural wood finish and a dark grip section near the base. The axe head is metallic, with a broad, crescent-shaped blade on one side and a pointed spike on the opposite side, both made of steel or iron. The blade is affixed perpendicularly to the handle, and the mounting appears robust and traditional. The handle is cylindrical and tapers slightly towards the end, with visible grip bands for improved handling. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative engravings or markings. The axe is well-suited for both display and practical use in urban or historical reenactment contexts.
description: A long-handled, medieval-style battle axe with a broad metal blade and a pointed spike, mounted on a straight wooden shaft with a dark grip section.
description_view_0: The axe is shown with its broad blade and spike clearly visible, highlighting the full profile of the head and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and spike, with the cylindrical handle and grip bands visible.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the opposite side of the axe head is visible, showing the blade and spike from the other direction.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again showing the thin profile of the blade and the straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a dark grip section, no curvature, and visible grip bands.
attribute_blade_design: Broad crescent-shaped single blade with a pointed spike on the opposite side, no beard, and a simple utilitarian profile.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted perpendicularly through the handle.
